title: Abolitionism Spreading to Campuses Across the Nation
author: Pate Anglin
orientation: A founder's recruitment piece announcing Students for Abolition (SFA), a Christian campus organization aimed at college students (especially young men) who want to confront abortion as a gospel and justice issue rather than through conventional pro-life slogans or pregnancy-center support.
audiences: College students considering starting an SFA chapter
Young Christian men weighing how to engage cultural evils
Pro-life supporters comparing abolitionism to mainstream pro-life activism
Campus ministry leaders evaluating new student orgs
Skeptics asking what Students for Abolition actually does

questions.question: What is Students for Abolition and where did it start?
questions.audience: Skeptics asking what Students for Abolition actually does
questions.answer: Students for Abolition (SFA) is a campus organization launched by the author and his roommate after a college ministry experience in which a campus socialist club challenged the humanity of the unborn. The chapter model is intentionally replicable, and SFA currently exists at East Tennessee State University and the University of Wisconsin-Stout, with more chapters in development.
questions.quote: Currently, Students for Abolition (SFA) is at East Tennessee State University and the University of Wisconsin-Stout, with more in the works at this very moment.

questions.question: Why did the founders feel a new student organization was necessary?
questions.audience: College students considering starting an SFA chapter
questions.answer: After watching a campus socialist club deny the humanity of the unborn, the founders felt morally compelled to publicly oppose what they viewed as a death cult on campus. They invoked Proverbs 24:11-12 as a charge to speak up for those being led to slaughter and resolved that someone had to act.
questions.quote:
Having just  
witnessed the humanity and value of the unborn questioned by a campus socialist club, we felt compelled to expose the unfruitful works of darkness propagated by this death cult across the university.

questions.question: How does SFA see its mission differently from typical pro-life campus activism?
questions.audience: Pro-life supporters comparing abolitionism to mainstream pro-life activism
questions.answer: SFA argues that chalking pro-life slogans on sidewalks and donating to pregnancy centers are insufficient responses if abortion is truly a modern-day holocaust. The organization wants students to publicly proclaim Christ's authority on campus rather than limit themselves to mild pro-life gestures.
questions.quote: Our students say they want to do more than merely draw pithy Pro-Life slogans with chalk on a sidewalk and give to local pregnancy centers. Because if we truly view the abortion issue as a modern-day holocaust, we must respond accordingly.

questions.question: What does SFA say about claims that abortion has been abolished in some states?
questions.audience: Pro-life supporters comparing abolitionism to mainstream pro-life activism
questions.answer: The article asserts that self-managed abortions remain legal everywhere, including states with pro-life supermajorities, and accuses pro-life organizations of bearing false witness when they claim otherwise while opposing bills of complete abolition. It frames this as lies and marketing gimmicks God will not bless.
questions.quote: Self-managed abortions are still legal in every state, including states with pro-life supermajorities and pro-life legislation. Meanwhile, many pro-life organizations bear false witness, claiming that there are states where abortion has been abolished, while simultaneously opposing bills of complete abolition.

questions.question: Why does the article specifically call out young Christian men?
questions.audience: Young Christian men weighing how to engage cultural evils
questions.answer: The author believes false claims about abortion already being abolished sap the zeal of young Christian men who would otherwise dedicate themselves to ending child sacrifice in their states. He invokes biblical figures like Josiah and David as models of repenting apathy and confronting idols.
questions.quote: We believe that these false claims dampen the fiery ambition of young Christian men, in particular, who might otherwise dedicate themselves to abolishing child sacrifice in their states.

questions.question: What role does Scripture play in how SFA argues against abortion?
questions.audience: Campus ministry leaders evaluating new student orgs
questions.answer: SFA treats Scripture as the primary, presupposed foundation for engaging the abortion issue, citing 2 Tim. 3:16-17 and Heb. 4:12. Scientific and other arguments are explicitly secondary tools rather than the main case.
questions.quote: At Students for Abolition, we encourage students to engage primarily with Scripture (2 Tim. 3:16-17; Heb. 4:12), presupposing that God’s Word is the foundation for truth, and refusing to sheath the Sword of the Spirit. We’ll still engage with science and other argumentation, but it’s our secondary weapon.

questions.question: How can a student start an SFA chapter at their school?
questions.audience: College students considering starting an SFA chapter
questions.answer: Interested students, especially young men aligned with the mission, can request an interview that covers their personal testimony and local church involvement. After approval they receive resources including SFA logos, discounted t-shirts, a sample constitution, a social media kit, a tabling package, and access to a community Discord.
questions.quote: Afterward, they’ll be given access to resources such as SFA logos, t-shirts at a great price, a sample SFA constitution, a social media kit, a tabling package, access to our community Discord, and more.

questions.question: What is the ultimate goal of Students for Abolition beyond campus activism?
questions.audience: Campus ministry leaders evaluating new student orgs
questions.answer: SFA frames its work as both gospel proclamation and justice advocacy, aiming to win glory for Christ through evangelism, education, and activism on the college campus. Engagement on abortion is paired with calling students to believe in Christ for forgiveness and salvation.
questions.quote:
As we continue to grow Students for Abolition, our mission remains clear: win glory for our Lord by advocating for the abolition of abortion through evangelism, education, and activism on the college  
campus.

related.title: Kristan Hawkins’ Flawed Reasoning vs Scripture
related.reason: Same author critiquing Students for Life leadership; directly contrasts with SFA's scripture-first stance.

related.title: Students For Life Advocates Legal Do-It-Yourself Abortions
related.reason: Reinforces the article's claim that self-managed abortions remain legal and that mainstream pro-life groups accept that reality.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Explains the abolitionist case against the incremental pro-life bills SFA accuses pro-life orgs of defending.

related.title: Abolitionist, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Foundational distinction between abolitionism and pro-life that SFA's campus pitch presupposes.

related.title: Abolitionist Theological Foundations
related.reason: Lays out the scripture-first theological framework SFA tells students to wield as their primary weapon.

related.title: Republican Establishment Plays Pro-Life Organizations Like a Fiddle
related.reason: Backs up SFA's accusation that pro-life organizations spread misleading claims about abortion having been abolished.

related.title: My Pro-Life-to-Abolitionist Conversion
related.reason: Personal-conversion narrative useful to students weighing the same shift SFA is asking them to make.
